2604.25895.title: Three Models of RLHF Annotation: Extension, Evidence, and Authority
2604.25895.paper_summary: Preference-based alignment methods, most prominently Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), use the judgments of human annotators to shape large language model behaviour. However, the normative role of these judgments is rarely made explicit. I distinguish three conceptual models of that role. The first is extension: annotators extend the system designers' own judgments about what outputs should be. The second is evidence: annotators provide independent evidence about some facts, whether moral, social or otherwise. The third is authority: annotators have some independent authority (as representatives of the broader population) to determine system outputs. I argue that these models have implications for how RLHF pipelines should solicit, validate and aggregate annotations. I survey landmark papers in the literature on RLHF and related methods to illustrate how they implicitly draw on these models, describe failure modes that come from unintentionally or intentionally conflating them, and offer normative criteria for choosing among them. My central recommendation is that RLHF pipeline designers should decompose annotation into separable dimensions and tailor each pipeline to the model most appropriate for that dimension, rather than seeking a single unified pipeline.
2604.25895.paper_summary_zh: 偏好基礎的對齊方法，最明顯的是帶有人類反饋的強化學習（RLHF），利用人類標註者的判斷來塑造大型語言模型的行為。<lb/>然而，這些判斷的規範性角色很少被明確表達。<lb/>我區分了這種角色的三個概念模型。<lb/>第一個是擴展：標註者擴展系統設計者對輸出應該是什麼的判斷。<lb/>第二個是證據：標註者提供有關某些事實的獨立證據，無論是道德的、社會的還是其他方面的。<lb/>第三個是權威：標註者擁有某種獨立的權威（作為更廣泛人群的代表）來決定系統的輸出。<lb/>我認為這些模型對於RLHF管道應如何徵求、驗證和聚合標註有著重要的影響。<lb/>我調查了RLHF及相關方法文獻中的標誌性論文，以說明它們如何隱含地依賴這些模型，描述由於無意或故意混淆它們而產生的失敗模式，並提供在它們之間選擇的規範標準。<lb/>我主要的建議是，RLHF管道設計者應將標註分解為可分離的維度，並根據最適合該維度的模型來量身定制每個管道，而不是尋求一個統一的管道。
2604.25895.author: Steve Coyne et.al.
